data.title: U.S. Commerce Secretary: Trump may forgive Musk but will not forget the negative impact
data.description: U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Trump may forgive Musk, but will not forget the negative impact he had after he left the administration. Musk was once one of the president's closest allies in the Trump administration. Lutnick said: "I think that's the kind of person that Elon Musk is. He did win the president's friendship and admiration, but when he left, he showed a very negative side of himself. And I don't think the president will forget that negativity. Trump will be friendly with Elon Musk in the future and will be friendly and kind because he is very affable, but do I think he will forget it? I don't think so." (The Hill)

data.title: Syz Capital Partner Richard Byworth Withdraws from Bitcoin ETFs and MSTR to Metaplanet
data.description: According to BitcoinTreasuries, Richard Byworth, a partner at Swiss hedge fund Syz Capital, is withdrawing funds from Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) and some Bitcoin ETF investments and starting to invest in Japanese Bitcoin treasury company Metaplanet. He expects the company's market performance to outperform Bitcoin's recent market trend by more than 50%.

data.title: Peter Schiff: Bitcoin's growth rate this year lags behind precious metals such as gold and silver, and it may have been in a long-term invisible bear market
data.description: Economist and cryptocurrency critic Peter Schiff published data on the X platform showing that BTC and ETH's year-to-date gains have lagged behind those of major precious metals: platinum is up 74%, silver is up 59%, palladium is up 44%, gold is up 43%, Ethereum is up 20%, and Bitcoin is up 16%. Peter Schiff also pointed out that Bitcoin has been in a long-term "hidden bear market," as its price, measured in gold, is currently 22% below its August 2025 high and 20% below its November 2021 high.

As of September 26, its total Bitcoin holdings had increased to 1,997.5 (Note: This number is pure holdings and does not include Bitcoin deposited by customers). In addition, its Bitcoin mining output this week was 108.3 BTC, but it sold 76.9 BTC during the same period.

data.title: Traders Express Skepticism Amid Market Volatility
data.description:
According to PANews, Adam, a macro researcher at Greeks.live, shared insights on the X platform regarding the current market sentiment. The group exhibits complex emotions, with traders positioning themselves on both sides. This includes selling call options at a strike price of $110,000 and buying put options at a strike price of $109,000. 

There is skepticism about the prevailing market optimism, with traders noting that 'CT's expectations for Q4 are overly optimistic' and describing the price movements as 'foolish price behavior.' As the weekend approaches, traders have observed significant volatility compression, making it 'difficult to see' substantial fluctuations. 

Several traders are actively managing their short option positions, including ETH call options and BTC put options, with plans to take profits before holding positions overnight.

data.title: Syz Capital Partner Shifts Investments to Japanese Bitcoin Firm
data.description: According to PANews, Richard Byworth, a partner at Swiss hedge fund Syz Capital, is reallocating investments from Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) and several Bitcoin ETFs to the Japanese Bitcoin treasury company Metaplanet. Byworth anticipates that Metaplanet's market performance could exceed Bitcoin's recent trends by over 50%.

data.title: Gate will launch Bitlight Labs (LIGHT) perpetual contract trading, leveraged lending, trading bots, copy trading, flash swaps, and fixed investment features.
data.description: According to an official announcement, Gate will launch Bitlight Labs (LIGHT) perpetual contracts trading at 7:30 PM (UTC+8) on September 27th, supporting 1-20x leverage. LIGHT margin lending will open at 9:10 PM (UTC+8) on September 27th. Additionally, LIGHT trading bots and copy trading will be available within one hour of the perpetual contract launch. LIGHT flash swap and fixed investment features will be available one hour after spot trading opens.

data.title: Zhiyun International Acquires Bitcoin on Regulated Hong Kong Platform
data.description: According to PANews, Zhiyun International, a company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, has announced that its wholly-owned subsidiary, Zhiyun Kuangshi Limited, has successfully opened a Bitcoin account on a regulated virtual asset trading platform in Hong Kong. The company has purchased over one Bitcoin using internal resources, with an average purchase price of approximately $118,000, equivalent to about HKD 918,000.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin slams EU’s chat control law as threat to digital privacy
data.description:
27-9-2025 – Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has publicly opposed the European Union’s proposed Chat Control legislation, labeling it a significant risk to digital privacy, as stated in a post on X. The legislation, which mandates tech platforms to scan private messages for illegal content, has drawn criticism for undermining end-to-end encryption. Buterin argued that creating [.
source: https://www.cointrackdaily.com/vitalik-buterin-slams-eus-chat-control-law-as-threat-to-digital-privacy/

data.title: Cryptocurrency Performance Trails Behind Precious Metals in 2025
data.description: According to PANews, economist and cryptocurrency critic Peter Schiff shared data on the X platform indicating that Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) have underperformed compared to major precious metals this year. Platinum has surged by 74%, silver by 59%, palladium by 44%, and gold by 43%, while Ethereum has risen by 20% and Bitcoin by 16%. Schiff also highlighted that Bitcoin has been in a prolonged 'stealth bear market,' as its price in gold terms is currently 22% lower than its peak in August 2025 and 20% below its high in November 2021.
